title: Witness deserialization: witness_data bytes → sapling::MerklePath
description:
Implement witness_to_merkle_path(witness_data: &[u8]) -> Result<sapling::MerklePath, TxBuildError>.

The witness_data stored in the DB is an IncrementalWitness<sapling::Node> serialized with its .write() method (using incrementalmerkletree's canonical format). This function reads it back.

Steps:
1. Spawn a research subagent to find the exact IncrementalWitness::read() signature in the incrementalmerkletree or sapling crate — locate the source at ~/.cargo/git/ or ~/GIT/librustzcash
2. Read the witness_data bytes using IncrementalWitness::read(cursor)
3. Call witness.path() to get Option<MerklePath> — return TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize if None (witness is not yet finalized)
4. Return the MerklePath

Defensive rules:
- Empty slice → TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize('empty witness data')
- Any read() error → TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize(msg) — never panic
- witness.path() returning None → TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize('witness has no path — may need more blocks')

Tests (oracle: write a known witness using IncrementalWitness::write, then round-trip through witness_to_merkle_path):
- Round-trip: build a synthetic IncrementalWitness, write to bytes, deserialize, verify root matches
- Empty bytes returns TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize
- Truncated bytes returns TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize (not panic)
- Corrupt bytes returns TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize (not panic)

Never use the code under test as its own oracle — use IncrementalWitness::write as the write oracle and verify the deserialized root independently.
acceptance_criteria: witness_to_merkle_path round-trips a synthetic witness; all error paths return TxBuildError not panic; zero allocations on the error paths
